data.title: Why Citi Sees Upside in These 2 AI Semiconductor Stocks Ahead of Earnings
data.description:
Citigroup is heading into the first-quarter semiconductor earnings season with a cautious view, but it sees near-term upside in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Analog Devices (ADI). The firm expects earnings-related catalysts and improving demand trends to support both stocks in the near term.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Citi also pointed to strong data center demand as a key driver for the sector. Data centers account for about 34% of total semiconductor demand, supported by ongoing investment in AI infrastructure. The firm expects capital spending by the five largest U.S. cloud companies to rise 69% in 2026, following a 79% increase in 2025. It also sees the total data center chip market reaching $731 billion by 2028.

AMD Sees Upside from Pricing and Share Gain

Citi expects AMD stock to rise in the near term, especially around its Q1 earnings. The positive view is driven by improving CPU demand, supported by growing use of AI across enterprise and cloud workloads. The firm also expects pricing to play a key role, noting that both AMD and Intel (INTC) plan to raise CPU prices starting in March and April.

At the same time, AMD continues to gain share in the server market, with revenue share rising to 41.3% in Q4 2025 from 39% in the prior quarter.

Citi also raised its 2026 earnings per share estimate slightly to $6.38, reflecting stronger demand trends. Looking ahead, Citi expects some softness in the second half of 2026. However, it believes AMD's PC business can still grow, supported by market share gains and a focus on higher-end products.

Nevertheless, the firm maintained a Neutral rating on the stock and lowered its price target to $248 from $260, still implying about 13% upside.

ADI Gains from Pricing Strength and Low Inventory

Citi sees near-term upside in Analog Devices. It maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $400 price target, implying about 22% upside.

The key driver is pricing. Industry checks show that Analog Devices is raising analog chip prices by 10% to 15% to offset higher input costs. This should help support margins and earnings in the coming quarters.

In addition, Citi's estimates for ADI are already above Wall Street consensus. This raises the chances of a positive earnings surprise, which could lift the stock.

At the same time, analog chip inventories appear to have bottomed, setting the stage for a recovery. With demand improving across industrial and data center markets, Analog Devices is well placed to benefit from the next growth cycle.

Which Is the Better AI Chip Stock, According to Analysts?

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to see which of the above-mentioned stocks analysts favor. According to analysts, AMD stock has the higher upside potential of 29.29%.

Investors can dig deeper to decide which AI chip stock best fits their strategy. Below is a screenshot for reference.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Broadcom (AVGO) Stock Rises on Google, Anthropic Chip Deals
data.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) shares rose 2.4% in after-hours trading yesterday after announcing multiyear chip deals with Alphabet (GOOGL) and AI startup Anthropic. The chip giant will develop and supply custom TPUs (Tensor Processing Units) and networking components for Google's AI data centers through 2031. A TPU is a specialized processor for machine learning. Additionally, Anthropic will gain access to 3.5 gigawatts of computing power from Google's AI processors, starting in 2027.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for NVDA bears

Why This Matters for Broadcom

These deals boost Broadcom's key role in custom chips (ASICs) and networking gear for Google's AI infrastructure. They also provide clear visibility into future AI demand, capacity planning, and revenue from its ASIC business.

The deals position Broadcom at the heart of training and deploying large models. Demand for custom silicon like TPUs has surged recently. Hyperscalers and startups are seeking efficient alternatives to Nvidia's (NVDA) costly GPUs (graphics processing units).

Broadcom Secures TPU Dominance

The long-term deal with Google covers custom TPUs for next-gen processors, plus networking and components for advanced AI racks. These components are vital for linking huge chip clusters that train and run neural networks like Google's Gemini. This partnership cements Broadcom as Google's main design partner for cutting-edge AI chips.

Anthropic Hits $30B Run Rate

Anthropic's new deal builds on its commitment to invest $50 billion in strengthening U.S. computing infrastructure. Demand for its AI model Claude has accelerated in 2026, driving its run-rate revenue to over $30 billion, up from about $9 billion at the end of 2025. The startup now has over 1,000 business clients spending more than $1 million annually, double the number from just two months ago.

Anthropic trains and runs Claude on a range of AI hardware, including Amazon Web Services' (AMZN) Trainium, Google TPUs, and Nvidia GPUs.

Is AVGO Stock a Good Buy?

On TipRanks, AVGO stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 27 Buys and three Hold ratings. The average Broadcom price target of $471.74 implies 50% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, AVGO shares have surged 104%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: UnitedHealth Stock (UNH) Jumps 8% after CMS Surprise. Let's See Who Owns It Ahead of Earnings
data.description:
UnitedHealth stock (UNH) jumped nearly 8% in after-hours trading on Monday after the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) finalized its 2027 Medicare Advantage (MA) payment update. The agency confirmed a 2.48% increase in payments to private insurers, an improvement from the near-flat rate proposed in January. The higher update helps ease concerns about rising medical costs and supports a better profit outlook.

Meanwhile, the company is set to report its first-quarter fiscal 2026 results before the market opens on Tuesday, April 21. Wall Street expects earnings per share (EPS) to decline 8% year over year to $6.65, while revenue is projected at $109.58 billion, roughly in line with the same period last year. With this in focus, let's take a closer look at who owns UNH stock.

Now, according to TipRanks' Ownership Tool, public companies and individual investors own 49.17% of UnitedHealth. They are followed by mutual funds, ETFs, other institutional investors, and insiders at 26.66%, 22.37%, 1.62%, and 0.18%, respectively.

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Rise as Hormuz Traffic Edges Higher ahead of Trump's April 7 Deadline
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed higher on Monday ahead of President Trump's April 7 deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • Trump warned that the U.S. would strike Iran's power plants and bridges if it doesn't reopen Hormuz by Tuesday evening. He also said there is a "good chance" a deal will be reached by then.
  • Iran rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal issued by the U.S., as well as Turkey, Egypt, and Pakistan as mediators. In response, Iran issued its own set of terms, which include the removal of sanctions, guidelines for safe passage through Hormuz, and a permanent end to the war.
  • Trump called Iran's terms "significant," but "not good enough."
  • The average price of a gallon of gas in the U.S. reached $4.12, the highest level since August 2022.
  • Traffic through Hormuz over the weekend reached its highest two-day level since early March as Iran granted individual safe-passage agreements to select countries.
  • Tesla (TSLA) closed lower after JPMorgan reiterated its price target of $145 and warned of lower financial estimates and execution risk.
  • Meta Platforms (META) is set to launch a new group of AI models developed under Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang, according to Axios.
  • Goldman Sachs raised its Netflix (NFLX) price target to $120 from $100, citing the company's user growth and content library.
  • Robinhood (HOOD) will partner with Bank of New York Mellon (BK) to launch an app for Trump accounts.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Consumer discretionary was the best-performing sector on Tuesday as optimism surrounding a U.S.-Iran deal and the reopening of Hormuz picked up ahead of Trump's deadline. A resolution of the war could lead to higher oil supply and lower inflation, driving consumer spending upwards. The sector also benefited from a rotation back into risk assets, led by Booking (BKNG), Starbucks (SBUX), and eBay (EBAY).

Several consumer discretionary stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Booking (BKNG)
  • Starbucks (SBUX)
  • eBay (EBAY)
  • Tractor Supply Company (TSCO)
  • Deckers Outdoor (DECK)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, the utilities sector faced the largest drawdown as investor capital rotated out of defensive sectors. A jump in Treasury yields over the past month has also pressured the rate-sensitive group, making utilities less attractive relative to bonds.

Notable stocks trading lower include:

  • NRG Energy (NRG)
  • Sempra (SRE)
  • Public Service Enterprise (PEG)
  • Dominion Energy (D)
  • NiSource (NI)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Casey's General Stores to Join S&P 500 – What It Means for Shareholders
data.description:
Convenience store chain Casey's General Stores (CASY) will join the coveted S&P 500 (SPX) index at Thursday's market open, April 9. It replaces Hologic Inc. (HOLX), due to its acquisition by affiliates of Blackstone Inc. (BX) and TPG Global (TPG), with the deal expected to complete today, April 7, 2026. S&P 500 additions often trigger share surges from index fund buying, expanding the investor base.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Additionally, DigitalOcean Holdings (DOCN) will replace CASY in the S&P MidCap 400, while Broadstone Net Lease (BNL) takes DOCN's spot in the S&P SmallCap 600. The S&P Dow Jones Indices announced the changes yesterday, pushing CASY shares over 4% in after-hours trading.

What's In It for Investors?

A company's inclusion in the SPX means greater liquidity and stability for stock investors. Both active and passive funds that track the S&P 500 index are required to add these companies to their portfolios, thus increasing demand. To qualify for inclusion, the company must have reported a profit in its most recent quarter and have cumulative profit in the trailing twelve months. Notably, CASY has surpassed profit expectations in all of the past eight quarters.

CASY stock has surged 12.5% since the company released solid Q3FY26 results on March 9. Diluted earnings per share (EPS) of $3.49 was much higher than the prior year's $2.33 and consensus of $3.00. Sales were mostly flat at $3.92 billion, missing expectations of $4.04 billion. Meanwhile, Inside same-store sales increased 4.0% compared to prior year and same-store fuel gallons were up 0.4%. CASY also pays a regular quarterly dividend of $0.57 per share, reflecting a yield of 0.39%.

Is CASY a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, CASY stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on seven Buys and six Hold ratings. The average Casey's General price target of $715.08 implies 4.4% downside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Cathie Wood Defies Delivery Slump and Bets $14M on Tesla's (TSLA) Dip, Dumps Teradyne Stock (TER)
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made new moves on Monday, April 6, 2026. They cut exposure to semiconductor testing firm Teradyne (TER) while buying more of Tesla (TSLA) on the dip, showing strong conviction in the EV stock despite recent weakness.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade TSLA

Wood Buys the Dip in Tesla Stock 

Tesla stock has remained under pressure after its weaker-than-expected Q1 delivery report. Shares fell more than 2% on Monday to close at $352.82, and Cathie Wood used the pullback as a clear buying opportunity.  She added 39,691 shares of Tesla across three ETFs — ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ), and ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX) — with a total value of about $14.3 million.

Following the delivery report, Wall Street remains split on the stock. On the bullish side, Wedbush's five-star-rated analyst Dan Ives maintained his Buy rating and $600 price target. He called the report weak but not surprising, noting that soft EV demand was already expected. Still, he sees strong upside from Tesla's work in AI and self-driving tech.

On the other hand, JPMorgan analyst Ryan Brinkman maintained a Sell rating with a $145 price target. He said Tesla's results have "collapsed" across key metrics and warned that investors should stay cautious given the risks and long wait for returns.

Even so, Wood's latest move shows she remains confident in the long-term story of the EV giant.

Is TSLA Stock a Buy Now? 

Tesla's stock has a consensus Hold rating among 32 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy, 11 Hold, and eight Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average TSLA price target of $393.97 implies 11.66% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: VTI and VOO: Why One Investor Is Doubling Down on These Vanguard ETFs
data.description:
Geopolitical uncertainty can create swings in the market, and the past month has offered a reminder of how quickly sentiment can shift. That said, with stocks rebounding over the past week, investors may be weighing whether to stay the course in equities or consider allocating part of their portfolio to more defensive assets such as bonds, precious metals, or certificates of deposit.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

For those looking for a middle ground, ETFs present a compelling alternative. While they don't fully sidestep market pullbacks, they do soften the blow by spreading exposure across a basket of assets, offering built-in diversification in a single, streamlined investment.

That's the approach investor Katie Brockman is leaning into right now, pointing to a pair of broad-based Vanguard funds as sensible options in the current setup – the Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI) and the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO).

The first "ultra-reliable" ETF she spotlights is VTI, which is made up of roughly 3,500 stocks representing a broad range of U.S.-listed equities. It has edged lower so far this year, sliding about 3%.

Looking beyond this dip, VTI has been a powerful tool for growth, with 1-, 3-, and 10-year gains of 14%, 57%, and 197%, respectively. Its beta of 1.01 suggests the ETF moves largely in sync with the market, while its expense ratio of 0.03% ranks among the lowest available.

While VTI may falter from time to time, alongside the broader market, Brockman argues that it remains a compelling long-term investment.

"Historically, the market itself has recovered from every single recession it's ever faced," the investor notes. "It is highly likely that the Total Stock Market ETF will also rebound from future downturns."
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: UnitedHealth (UNH) or Humana (HUM): Which Stock Is the Better Buy After CMS' Surprise Rate Hike
data.description:
UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) shares are climbing nearly 7% in after-hours trading, while Humana (NYSE:HUM) is rallying close to 12%, as investors react to a Medicare Advantage update that turned out far better than many had anticipated and reshaped the near-term outlook for managed care providers.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The move follows the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services' final notice on 2027 payment rates, which delivered a roughly 2.48% average increase to plans, coming in well above the earlier proposal and easing concerns that reimbursement pressure could weigh on margins across the sector. That shift matters a great deal for companies like UnitedHealth and Humana, where Medicare Advantage represents a meaningful portion of the business and plays a central role in profitability.

In recent months, investors had been bracing for a tougher update, particularly given ongoing debates around risk adjustment changes and the potential for stricter reimbursement dynamics. Instead, the final outcome suggests a more measured approach, one that gives insurers additional breathing room and improves visibility into earnings over the next few years. The stronger rate environment also helps offset lingering concerns around utilization trends and medical cost pressures that had been hanging over the group.

Molly T. Turco, a healthcare policy analyst at TD Cowen, frames the outcome as a policy-driven decision that leans in favor of stability rather than immediate recalibration. She notes that "this increase is largely due to the policy decision to not update the risk adjustment model," pointing to a choice that softens what could have been a more restrictive reimbursement framework and postpones a more challenging transition for insurers.

Turco also interprets the final notice as carrying a broader message about policymakers' priorities at this stage. In her view, "the meaningfully improved final update indicates CMS, or more likely the White House, was swayed by concerns" about potential disruptions in the Medicare Advantage market, suggesting that maintaining continuity took precedence over pushing through more aggressive changes right away.

When it comes to earnings, Turco sees the implications as meaningfully positive, particularly if plans are able to translate the improved rate environment into margin recovery. The analyst points out that even incremental changes can have a sizable effect, noting that every 50 basis point improvement in Medicare Advantage margins could drive roughly 3% to 5% earnings upside for companies like UnitedHealth and Humana. At the same time, she cautions that the ultimate benefit will depend on execution, explaining that insurers will need to balance pricing, coding intensity, and medical cost management to fully capture the opportunity embedded in the updated rates.

UNH Stock Is a Better Buy

To round it out with Wall Street's view, the contrast between the two names is fairly clear, and it leans in favor of UnitedHealth. From a consensus standpoint, UnitedHealth boasts a Strong Buy rating, backed by 18 Buys and just 4 Holds, with no Sell ratings at all, alongside an average price target of $368.15 that implies about 30% upside from current levels. Humana, by comparison, holds a more cautious Hold consensus rating, with only 5 Buys against 12 Holds and 2 Sells, and a more modest implied upside of ~13% as the 12-month average price target of $207.18 suggests.
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook Is Selling Company Stock
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) CEO Tim Cook is selling the company's stock as the share price languishes.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

According to a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), Cook sold $16.5 million of company stock on April 2 of this year. The sales were executed in multiple transactions with prices ranging from $251.25 to $256.00 per share.

According to the SEC filing, Cook sold a total of 5,087 Apple shares under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan that is used to eliminate the perception that company executives are buying and selling stock based on insider information. Cook's latest sales come with AAPL stock down 5% this year an treading water around $255 per share.

Tim Cook's Apple Holdings

As CEO of Apple, Tim Cook continues to own 3.28 million shares of company stock, valued at $847.86 million based on current market prices. Much of Cook's compensation at Apple is based on stock awards received for achieving certain milestones with the company.

There have been rumors that Cook plans to retire and stepdown as CEO of Apple later this year. However, Cook recently shot down those rumors, saying in a media interview that he has not said anything publicly about his tenure leading Apple, which began in 2011 following the death of Steve Jobs.

Is AAPL Stock a Buy?

Apple stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 24 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy, nine Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AAPL price target of $304.66 implies 22.35% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Stock Market Futures Mixed as Middle East Tensions Cloud Outlook
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed on Monday night as investors weighed rising Middle East tensions, with a focus on Donald Trump's Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.19% and 0.11%, respectively, at 9:00 p.m. EDT on April 6, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures were up 0.08%.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade QQQ with leverage

Crude prices moved slightly higher, with Brent crude (CM:BZ) up 0.48% to $110.23 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) rose 0.78% to $113.32 per barrel.

In the regular trading session, major indices closed modestly higher after President Donald Trump said Iran was "an active, willing participant" in talks to end the conflict, while warning that the U.S. would strike Iran's power plants and bridges if Iran does not reopen Hormuz by Tuesday evening. The Nasdaq, the S&P 500, and the Dow rose 0.5%, 0.4%, and 0.4%, respectively.

Looking ahead, traders will be watching February's durable goods orders and the consumer credit report on Tuesday.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
